rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section to support the evaluability account that more affective evaluations are less sensitive to quantitative scope. The citing authors explicitly use this logic, alongside related Hsee work, to justify a key modeling assumption (the MRP relation between reaction and vicarious utility) that drives their hypotheses about agents' impatience. This is more than a passing literature mention because it informs the conceptual setup of the model, even though no specific methods or data are reused.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Hsee et al. (2009) as part of the evaluability foundation for its self-other intertemporal choice model. That evaluability logic is used to motivate the assumed shape difference between reaction and vicarious utility functions, which underpins the paper's main predictions.
